summary.predicted_pattern: Recessive yellow (e/e) OR sable (ay/ay) — cannot distinguish
summary.predicted_white: Minor white markings possible (KIT het, MITF het)
summary.caveat: MC1R (E locus) cannot be resolved from this dataset — COSMO was processed from Parker array data rather than GLIMPSE2 WGS imputation, and the actual p.Arg306* coding position (chr5:63922309) has insufficient BAM coverage. ASIP (A locus) sable/agouti requires structural variant analysis. A dedicated commercial coat color test (Embark, Wisdom Panel, or Optimal Selection) uses specific diagnostic probes for these variants and would give definitive results. The K, B, D, M loci are better supported.
summary.irf4_note: Cosmo carries a homozygous deletion of IRF4 (chr35:714,202–738,113 kb). IRF4 is a transcription factor that regulates MITF expression and melanocyte differentiation. Loss of IRF4 in mice and dogs is associated with coat colour lightening independent of the classical E/K/A pathway. This deletion may contribute to Cosmo's beige/cream phenotype even if the MC1R/ASIP genotype is not e/e or ay/ay.

E.name: Extension locus
E.role: Controls whether eumelanin (black/brown) or phaeomelanin (red/yellow) is produced

alleles_reference.e: Recessive red/yellow — overrides A and K loci

E.interpretation: MC1R (E locus) cannot be reliably called from this dataset. The actual MC1R p.Arg306* coding position in canFam4 is chr5:63,922,309 (gene chr5:63,922,271–63,923,224, minus strand) — this position is not N-masked but has insufficient BAM coverage in this sample (2 reads, no alt allele detected). The Dog10K imputation panel does not include this position; COSMO was processed from Parker array data rather than GLIMPSE2 WGS imputation. The two observed variants (chr5:63685948 and chr5:63688726) are near-MC1R array positions of uncertain biological relevance. Given that Cosmo is beige/cream, e/e (recessive yellow) remains the leading hypothesis. A dedicated commercial test (Embark, Wisdom Panel) with targeted diagnostic probes would give a definitive result.
E.phenotype_contribution: e/e suspected (beige phenotype) — actual MC1R p.Arg306* coding position (chr5:63922309) has insufficient coverage to confirm

K.name: Dominant black locus
K.role: KB suppresses A-locus expression; ky/ky allows A-locus patterns to show
K.interpretation: Hom_alt splice_acceptor_variant in CBD103. AF=0.39 suggests this may be a common population variant rather than KB. KB allele (signal peptide Cys3Tyr) not found. Provisional call: ky/ky — A locus expressed.
K.phenotype_contribution: If ky/ky: A locus determines coat pattern

A.name: Agouti locus
A.role: Controls pigment switching pattern when K locus allows (ky/ky)
A.interpretation: The ASIP locus distinguishes ay (sable/fawn), at (tan points), and a (recessive black). The regulatory region driving ay expression is N-masked at the gene start in canFam4. Parker array markers across chr24:24,000,000–24,200,000 are all 0/0 (homozygous reference) in Cosmo, which tentatively argues against ay/ay if those markers tag the ay haplotype — but this is uncertain since the causal regulatory variants may not be represented. If E locus is E/- (not e/e), then the A locus phenotype would be visible: given the all-over beige colour, ay/ay (sable) would be more consistent than at/at (tan points) or a/a (recessive black).
A.phenotype_contribution: If ay: sable/fawn coat pattern (when ky/ky)

B.name: Brown locus
B.role: Converts black eumelanin to brown (chocolate/liver). Recessive.
B.interpretation: Heterozygous variants in TYRP1 flanking region. No hom_alt coding variants found. Consistent with B/b (carrier of chocolate) or B/B (non-chocolate). No chocolate predicted.

D.name: Dilution locus
D.role: Dilutes eumelanin or phaeomelanin. d/d gives blue (from black) or isabella (from brown).

observed_variants.source: VEP functional — very common, likely benign

D.interpretation: Heterozygous variants in MLPH. The AF=0.91 missense is almost certainly a benign population polymorphism. No known d1 (splice site) allele detected. Cosmo is NOT dilute.

M.name: Merle locus
M.role: SINE insertion causes merle pattern. Semi-dominant; double merle causes white/vision problems.
M.interpretation: Merle (PMEL SINE insertion) requires structural variant analysis not performed here. No merle pattern expected in poodle/cocker ancestry. Provisional: m/m.

S.name: White spotting locus
S.role: Controls white spotting/piebald. Regulatory SW allele upstream of MITF.
S.interpretation: One rare MITF missense variant (het, AF=0.06). The SW allele associated with piebald spotting in dogs is a regulatory variant ~20kb upstream. Heterozygous rare missense may contribute to white spotting but is uncertain.

W.name: White locus
W.role: KIT loss-of-function causes white spotting (pied) or flash markings in some breeds

observed_variants.source: VEP functional — nearly fixed in all dogs, benign

W.interpretation: Heterozygous KIT stop_gained (AF=0.33 — common in dog population, may be tolerated). The hom_alt KIT missense at AF=0.98 is nearly fixed across all dogs — benign background variant. KIT stop het may contribute to white flash/ticking.
